A.does: Executes a search by maintaining a set of active NFA states in parallel across each input byte (Thompson NFA simulation), advancing all live threads simultaneously via epsilon closure so every possible match path is explored without backtracking.

B.does: Executes a search via explicit stack-based backtracking, using a `Visited` bitset indexed by (StateID, haystack-offset) pairs to prevent re-visiting any state+position combination, bounding worst-case behavior at the cost of O(N×M) memory.

comparison: PikeVM is preferred for unbounded or large inputs since it runs in O(n) time regardless of haystack size, while BoundedBacktracker is preferred for short haystacks where its visited-state bitset fits in memory, as its simpler single-path execution is often faster in practice.
